core.when: When calculating returns using round_trip_analysis functions
core.action: Verify that return calculations use pnl/invested_capital as the denominator rather than pnl/portfolio_value; the formula divides profit by the capital committed to the trade, not the total portfolio value

consequence.description: Using the wrong denominator for return calculations produces misleading performance metrics; a $100 profit on a $1000 position shows 10% return vs potentially 50% of portfolio value, causing incorrect strategy evaluation and potentially deploying capital to underperforming strategies

evidence_refs.summary: 6b55da7 Derived from BD: BD-013

core.when: When configuring market impact assumptions for capacity analysis
core.action: Verify that max_bar_consumption=0.2 (20% of daily volume) matches the actual trading volume and liquidity profile of the target assets; adjust the threshold based on asset class and trading style

consequence.description: Default bar consumption of 20% of daily volume may underestimate market impact for large orders in less liquid securities, causing execution shortfalls in live trading that invalidate backtest results and reduce realized strategy returns

evidence_refs.summary: 6b55da7 Derived from BD: BD-025

core.when: When calling turnover extraction and position stacking functions in sequence
core.action: Extract turnover from dollar-denominated positions BEFORE converting to percentages; do not reverse this ordering or pass percentages to turnover or dollars to stacking functions — maintain the sequential contract: dollar positions → turnover extraction → percent conversion → stacking

consequence.description: Passing percentages directly to turnover or dollars to stacking causes silent calculation failures or incorrect attribution results since the functions expect different unit types; errors accumulate without obvious failure signals

evidence_refs.summary: 6b55da7 Derived from BD: BD-125

validation_threshold: Verify positions passed to turnover function are in dollars and positions passed to stacking function are in percentages before each call

core.when: When processing data in data_preprocessing stage
core.action: Assume the framework provides point-in-time (PIT) data availability — the framework does not implement PIT data handling, and data may contain lookahead bias if not properly validated before use

consequence.description: Without point-in-time data handling, backtests may use information that was not available at the time of decision, introducing lookahead bias that causes live trading returns to fall far below backtested results

evidence_refs.summary: 6b55da7 Derived from BD: BD-GAP-003

core.when: When normalizing return series for cross-strategy or cross-period comparison
core.action: Use first-value normalization (returns.iloc[0]) with starting_value=1 to create comparable series starting at 1.0; verify the first non-null value is used as the normalization denominator

consequence.description: Using different normalization methods produces incomparable return series; without consistent first-value normalization, cross-strategy and cross-period performance analysis produces misleading comparisons that may lead to incorrect strategy selection

evidence_refs.summary: 6b55da7 Derived from BD: BD-068

core.when: When classifying a trading strategy as intraday in the backtesting framework
core.action: Use position_count/transactions_per_day < 0.25 threshold for intraday detection — do not modify this threshold without re-validating against actual trading patterns

consequence.description: Setting threshold too high (>0.25) misclassifies intraday strategies as positional, causing intraday position adjustments to be ignored and producing inflated returns; setting too low (<0.25) over-corrects, distorting genuine positional strategies

evidence_refs.summary: 6b55da7 Derived from BD: BD-048

core.when: When using the detect_intraday function with threshold parameter
core.action: Verify the 0.25 threshold produces correct classifications by comparing against known intraday strategies — higher thresholds (0.5) classify more strategies as intraday; lower thresholds (0.1) require sustained same-day trading

consequence.description: Using an unvalidated threshold causes systematic misclassification of strategy types, leading to incorrect position estimation and unreliable performance attribution in backtest results

evidence_refs.summary: 6b55da7 Derived from BD: BD-048

core.when: When generating sub-tear sheets after position extraction has already applied intraday correction
core.action: Re-apply intraday estimation when estimate_intraday=True is set by default — positions have already been corrected upstream and re-estimation introduces redundant adjustments

consequence.description: Re-applying intraday estimation to already-corrected positions double-adjusts positions, systematically distorting the equity curve and creating inconsistent results between main and sub-tear sheets

evidence_refs.summary: 6b55da7 Derived from BD: BD-029

core.when: When processing positions through multiple pipeline stages
core.action: Track whether intraday estimation has been applied upstream and set estimate_intraday=False to preserve original data integrity in subsequent stages

consequence.description: Without tracking estimation status, subsequent pipeline stages may inadvertently re-apply estimation, causing position drift and corrupted performance metrics that accumulate silently across report generations

evidence_refs.summary: 6b55da7 Derived from BD: BD-029

core.when: When using estimate_intraday with the default EOD_hour=23 setting for A-share or extended-hours trading
core.action: Verify EOD_hour=23 aligns with your market's after-hours trading session — this setting treats 23:00 as end-of-day snapshot but does NOT provide true intraday granularity; positions are attributed to the calendar day before market open

consequence.description: Using EOD_hour=23 for markets without after-hours trading attributes positions to the wrong calendar day, causing date misalignment in performance attribution and potentially misleading overnight position risk calculations

evidence_refs.summary: 6b55da7 Derived from BD: BD-105

core.when: When initializing position data in the backtesting framework
core.action: Set pos_in_dollars=True for dollar-based position values (market value tracking) or pos_in_dollars=False for share-quantity positions — do not mix formats without explicit conversion

consequence.description: Passing share quantities when pos_in_dollars=True causes exposure calculations to use raw share counts as dollar amounts, massively overstating position sizes and producing backtest returns that are completely inconsistent with actual capital requirements

evidence_refs.summary: 6b55da7 Derived from BD: BD-108

core.action: Verify pos_in_dollars flag matches the actual input data format — when True, framework expects dollar amounts; when False, expects share quantities requiring price multiplication for dollar-based exposure calculations

evidence_refs.summary: 6b55da7 Derived from BD: BD-108

core.when: When implementing or refactoring backtesting validation logic
core.action: Assume the framework provides backtest overfitting protection — no overfitting detection mechanism exists; strategies can be tuned to historical data without safeguards

consequence.description: Without overfitting protection, optimized strategies appear profitable in backtesting but fail in live trading; walk-forward analysis shows Sharpe ratios typically collapse by 60-80% when overfitted strategies are deployed

core.when: When running backtest optimization for strategy parameter tuning
core.action: Implement walk-forward analysis with out-of-sample testing, enforce minimum backtest-to-parameter ratio (at least 10:1 observations per parameter), and require at least 2 out-of-sample periods for validation

consequence.description: Without overfitting protection, optimized strategies appear profitable in backtesting but fail in live trading; walk-forward analysis shows Sharpe ratios typically collapse by 60-80% when overfitted strategies are deployed

evidence_refs.summary: 6b55da7 Derived from BD: BD-GAP-009

core.when: When implementing optimization routines for strategy parameter search
core.action: Assume the framework enforces optimization constraint completeness — no built-in constraints exist for parameter bounds, optimization depth, or search space limits; unbounded optimization is permitted

consequence.description: Unbounded optimization allows unlimited parameter search that systematically fits noise in historical data, creating strategies that appear optimal in backtests but have no predictive value in live trading

evidence_refs.summary: 6b55da7 Derived from BD: BD-GAP-010

core.when: When setting up optimization runs for strategy parameter tuning
core.action: Define explicit parameter bounds (minimum 5 data points per parameter level), limit optimization iterations, require out-of-sample validation pass before accepting optimization results, and reject any parameter set that shows >30% performance degradation on holdout data

consequence.description: Unbounded optimization allows unlimited parameter search that systematically fits noise in historical data, creating strategies that appear optimal in backtests but have no predictive value in live trading
